channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Americans rate Trump's cabinet picks in new poll
item.description: While a majority of American voters questioned in a new Fox News poll say they are hopeful about the re-election of former President Donald Trump, they are divided when it comes to the nominees who will likely serve in his upcoming second administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump border czar meeting with NYC Mayor Adams despite sanctuary city status
item.description: Incoming border czar Tom Homan will meet with New York City Mayor Eric Adams in NYC where they will discuss the city’s ongoing migrant crisis and ways of deporting criminal illegal migrants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What Americans think about Hegseth, Gabbard and key Trump Cabinet picks AP-NORC poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As several of President-elect Donald Trump’s choices for high-level positions in his incoming administration face scrutiny on Capitol Hill, a poll from The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research shows that Americans have their own doubts.
item.dc:creator: LINLEY SANDERS and MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A South Texas lawmaker's 15-year fight for a Rio Grande Valley law school
item.description: Advocates say a law school in one of the state's poorest regions would exponentially help communities with legal aid.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Berenice Garcia, KPRC 2

item.title: Future of Democratic Party rest in hands of one of these candidates
item.description: While the average American may not know who the chair of the Democratic National Committee is, the role has a direct impact on their lives.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Trump taps immigration hard-liner Kari Lake as head of Voice of America
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump says he’s picking Kari Lake as director of Voice of America, installing a staunch loyalist who ran unsuccessfully for Arizona governor and a Senate seat to head the congressionally funded broadcaster that provides independent news reporting around the world.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert And Jonathan J. Cooper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: House passes nearly $1 trillion defense spending bill, adding to US debt of $36 trillion
item.description: The U.S. House passed a mostly bipartisan bill to spend nearly $1 trillion on national defense, adding to the already growing $36 trillion national debt.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Person accused of accosting Rep. Nancy Mace at Capitol pleads not guilty to assault charge
item.description: A person accused of accosting U.S. Rep. Nancy Mace in a Capitol Office building has pleaded not guilty to a misdemeanor assault charge.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Garland lauds 'principled' Wray for 'integrity and skill' after resignation announcement
item.description: AG Garland praises FBI Director Wray's multi-decade career as a civil servant and U.S. prosecutor, including U.S. assistant AG and head of the Justice Department's Criminal Division.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Secretary of State Blinken is returning to the Mideast in his latest diplomatic foray
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Antony Blinken is returning to the Middle East this week on his 12th visit since the Israel-Hamas war erupted last year but his first since the ouster of Syrian President Bashar Assad.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics: Wray To Go
item.description: the Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump transition, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democratic senators urge Biden to act on temporary protections for migrants
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democratic senators are urging President Joe Biden to extend temporary protections for migrants in the U.S. before he leaves office, warning that millions of people could be forced to return to unsafe countries once President-elect Donald Trump retakes the White House.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, COLLEEN LONG and GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Newsom sets special election dates for vacant 32nd District Assembly seat
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom announced a special election date for the vacant 32nd District Assembly.
item.dc:creator: Jose Franco, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FBI Director Wray says he intends to resign before Trump takes office in January
item.description: FBI Director Christopher Wray says he plans to resign at the end of President Joe Biden’s term in January.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Christopher Wray to resign as FBI director ahead of Trump takeover
item.description: FBI Director Christopher Wray announced Wednesday he plans to resign from the agency, leaving his post ahead of President-Trump's apparent plans to fire him.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Trump, team trail Fauci on trust as medical information source: Survey
item.description: The latest poll from Axios/Ipsos American Health Index shows that 45 percent of Americans say they either have “a great deal” or “a fair amount” of trust in information regarding health topics coming from Fauci.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court dismisses Nvidia’s appeal in securities lawsuit
item.description: The Supreme Court on Wednesday dismissed an appeal from Nvidia in a securities lawsuit against the chipmaking giant, allowing the case to move forward. The court dismissed the appeal as “improvidently granted” in an unsigned, one-sentence opinion without explanation.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Rising US-Mexico tension a game of optics, analyst says
item.description: Change is coming to the border on Jan. 20, when President-elect Trump takes office. How profound and lasting such change might be remains to be seen, but one thing is certain: Optics will play a big role in Washington, D.C., and in Mexico City.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Cornyn warns Hegseth faces 'unpleasant' confirmation hearing
item.description: Sen. John Cornyn (R-Texas) says Pete Hegseth, President-elect Trump’s pick to head the Department of Defense, faces a nasty Senate confirmation hearing that would likely be uglier than the battle over Supreme Court Justice Brett Kavanaugh in 2018.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Bolton: Senators ‘won’t escape history’s judgment’ if they vote to confirm Patel to FBI
item.description: John Bolton, President-elect Trump’s former national security adviser, is warning that Republican senators will be judged harshly by history if they confirm Kash Patel as the next FBI director.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hegseth makes play to win hard-to-get GOP centrists
item.description: Pete Hegseth is looking to win over a pair of key Republican moderates as he tries to keep his hopes of leading the Defense Department from being derailed. The former Fox News host huddled with Sen. Lisa Murkowski (R-Alaska) on Tuesday and is set to do the same on Wednesday with Sen.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Trump, team trail Fauci on trust as medical information source: Survey
item.description: Trump and other Republicans have criticized Fauci, who was the face of the first Trump and Biden administration’s response against COVID-19, over his handling of the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kimberly Guilfoyle gets Greece as consolation prize after Donald Trump Jr. dumps her: Source
item.description: Kimberly Guilfoyle has been tapped to be the U.S. Ambassador to Greece in the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: DFL, Republican senators weigh in on state budget forecast
item.description: The state’s latest economic forecast, revealed Dec. 4, predicts less revenue than expected and higher-than-expected costs for some government programs. MPR News host Emily Bright spoke with state Sens.
item.dc:creator: Emily Bright and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Jacob Frey vetoes 2025 budget approved by Minneapolis City Council
item.description: Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey announced Wednesday afternoon that he is vetoing the 2025 budget approved by the city council late Tuesday night. The budget had passed with enough votes to override a veto.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: Biden approves national security memo on China, Iran, North Korea and Russia ahead of Trump's return
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden has approved a new national security memorandum ahead of Donald Trump's return to the White House that could serve as a road map for the incoming administration as it looks to counter growing cooperation among China, Iran, North Korea and Russia, the White House
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Increased Christianity in schools opens the door to Satanic Temple education programs
item.description: The in-school offerings are in addition to the After School Satan Clubs, which launched in 2020 and have risen in popularity among parents seeking alternatives to religious clubs.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's withdrawn nominees latest in a long line of Cabinet members who never were
item.description: Many presidents face failed Cabinet picks so when former Rep. Matt Gaetz withdrew from consideration for President-elect Donald Trump's Cabinet, it wasn't unprecedented, though Trump's style has been.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore, MPR News

item.title: Congress races toward fast-approaching government shutdown deadline
item.description: Congress has just 10 days until government funding is set to run out, and lawmakers don’t have a deal to keep the lights on during the holidays. Members on both sides of the aisle expect the government will stay open past the Dec. 20 shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: Nevada adds blockchain security to prevent repeat of 'fake electors' mischief
item.description: Nevada has integrated blockchain technology into its election certification process to add even more security to its procedures, the Nevada Secretary of State's Office announced on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court rejects challenge to Boston's school admissions policy
item.description: The court's action was the second time the justices declined to intervene in an admissions program based on geography since their 2023 ruling invalidating affirmative action in higher education.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: Social Security beneficiaries at risk of delays amid GOP funding fight
item.description: Social Security beneficiaries could face longer wait times for service next year unless Congress agrees to the White House budget office’s request to increase funding for the Social Security Administration (SSA), the administration that runs the program is warning.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Mayoral hopeful Michael Blake discusses vision for NYC
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – As we approach 2025, the new year marks a new election cycle. Political consultant Michael Blake joins PIX on Politics to discuss his decision to join the long list of Democrats competing with Mayor Eric Adams in next year’s mayoral primary.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: NYC mayor will not completely rule out switch to Republican Party
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- New York Democratic Mayor Eric Adams sent shockwaves through the political scene Friday morning after refusing to rule out running for another term as a Republican.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Walz says he was 'a little surprised' by election loss
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), Vice President Kamala Harris' running mate, reflected on the Democratic Party's loss in the presidential election, saying he was "a little surprised" by the outcome.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez seeks to lead Democrats on Oversight panel
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) on Friday formally launched a bid to lead Democrats on the Oversight and Accountability Committee in the next Congress — a powerful seat that will play a prominent role in the party's effort to push back against President Trump in his second term.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota U.S. Attorney Andy Luger to step down ahead of Trump transition
item.description: U.S. Attorney Andy Luger is stepping down soon as the top federal law enforcement official in Minnesota. As is custom for political appointees, he’s resigning to make way for a yet-to-be-announced successor chosen by incoming President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News
